description_long: This traffic barricade is constructed from a series of evenly spaced vertical metal bars, all connected to a sturdy horizontal frame. The top corners of the frame are angled, giving the barricade a slightly trapezoidal appearance. The entire structure is supported by two robust, outward-angled legs that provide stability on flat surfaces. The metal is finished in a dark, matte color, likely powder-coated for weather resistance. There are no visible signs, lights, or reflective markings on the surface. The open design allows visibility through the barricade while effectively restricting passage.
description: A dark metal traffic barricade with vertical bars, angled corners, and two sturdy support legs. The open design restricts passage but allows visibility.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full array of vertical bars and the angled top corners.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and the outward-angled support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the vertical bars and frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and support legs.
